summary.intent: Implement task #204: the framework-agnostic Highlight utility component for ars-ui, which splits text into highlighted/non-highlighted chunks with three match strategies and Unicode-aware case folding.
summary.outcome: PR #647 pushed with the full Highlight implementation, three rounds of spec/impl drift audits all landed, a reusable post-implementation-audit skill added to CLAUDE.md as a mandatory step, and five PR review comments addressed with corrected semantics and an extra fmt-fix commit.

repo: ars-components declares `ars-i18n = { default-features = false }`, so any ICU4X API requires an explicit feature flag on the consuming crate — the feature-free build compiles but the gated module is simply absent, not degraded
CLAUDE.md is a symlink to AGENTS.md; edits must target AGENTS.md directly to persist
.claude/skills is a symlink to .agents/skills; canonical skill paths belong under .agents/
CI runs nightly rustfmt, which can reformat code that local stable rustfmt considers valid — always run `cargo +nightly fmt` before pushing
The spec template (foundation/10-component-spec-template.md §4.1.2) mandates Part/Api/ConnectApi even for function-based components; Highlight spec's 'rather than' language was wrong

Parametric anatomy slots (parts whose attributes depend on runtime data, e.g., per-chunk highlighted bool) must NOT get a Part enum variant — expose them via a parameterised Api method; this is now documented in foundation/10
cargo-mutants timeout exits count as caught mutations per CLAUDE.md — infinite-loop mutations from removing loop-advance operators are legitimate catches to document in mutants.toml, not suppress

Coverage routing: native crates use `cargo llvm-cov`; wasm/adapter crates must use `cargo xtask coverage wasm` — bare llvm-cov reports 0% for wasm-gated paths, making it look like a gap rather than an unmeasured path
`cargo insta test … --unreferenced=reject` is the correct way to verify no orphan snapshots exist after accepting or deleting snapshots
The snapshot-reviewed label must be re-applied for any push that adds or modifies .snap files, not just the initial PR open

code.finding: Existing precedent for locale-aware case-insensitive matching; was originally cited to justify reusing to_lowercase, but ultimately the eszett gap made case_fold the correct choice instead
Canonical pattern for snapshot_attrs helper + assert_snapshot! — all new utility components follow this structure
to_lowercase wraps CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string — does NOT expand ß to ss; only the new case_fold (TR21 fold_string) produces the ß↔ss/SS equivalence required for case-insensitive matching

New module; must be gated with #[cfg(feature = "i18n")] in mod.rs because the entire correctness contract (Turkic, eszett, Greek) depends on the ICU4X backend — a feature-off fallback to str::to_lowercase violates the MUST spec language and was removed
Timeout-producing mutations (e.g., removing the offset increment inside a scan loop) should be documented as equivalent here rather than left as unexplained timeouts in CI output

workflow: TDD ordering is enforced by CLAUDE.md: write tests first (expected to fail), then implement; running them before the impl confirms they actually fail and thus test real behaviour
cargo xci is the mandatory full pre-PR gate — run it before every push, not just before the first one
Three-round post-implementation-audit flow (spec drift → iterative 'anything else?' × 2 clean rounds → test coverage audit) is now codified as a mandatory skill invocation in CLAUDE.md step 7

German eszett ß↔ss equivalence requires TR21 case folding (CaseMapper::fold_string), not locale-aware lowercasing (CaseMapper::lowercase_to_string) — to_lowercase preserves ß; a test that only checks SS→ss will pass even when the contract is violated
When an initial plan assumption ('no Cargo.toml changes') turns out wrong during implementation, document the deviation explicitly in the summary rather than silently deviating
Mutation testing should be re-run after any significant algorithmic refactor — the mutants.toml skip list may need updating as the algorithm changes
Adapter feature wiring (e.g. ars-leptos/icu4x propagating ars-components/i18n) is easy to miss during implementation; check all adapter Cargo.toml files when adding a feature-gated module

friction: Plan incorrectly stated 'no Cargo.toml changes' — ars-components uses ars-i18n with default-features = false, making the i18n feature flag unavoidable; assumption was not verified before planning

Initial case-folding approach (to_lowercase) silently violated the German eszett contract; the initial test only exercised the trivial uppercase→lowercase direction and passed despite the ß→ss direction being broken; the bug was only caught during the audit round
CI nightly rustfmt reformatted a match expression that local stable rustfmt had left untouched, requiring a corrective commit after the PR was already pushed and CI had run

Two algorithmic bugs surfaced in PR reviews that the 44 unit tests and 1000-case proptest had not caught: (1) Fuzzy matching consumed only one fold-expanded byte per source char instead of all bytes of the fold expansion, causing İ to highlight only i and miss the combining dot; (2) Contains byte-map did not track source_end separately from source_start, making overlapping fold expansions produce incorrect original-text ranges
cargo xci output is only available via a background task file — there is no streaming progress indicator, making it unclear whether the job is still running or has silently exited

The skill-creator external analyzer flagged six issues in the initial SKILL.md draft, requiring a second edit pass; one finding (both-spec-and-impl-wrong escalation path) was a genuine gap that had been overlooked
open_items: Adapter wrapper components (Leptos and Dioxus implementations of Highlight) are explicitly out of scope for this task — separate issues to be filed
Issue #204 remains open on the project board until PR #647 passes CI and merges

Promoting the 'parametric anatomy slot' pattern to foundation/10-component-spec-template.md as a first-class concept was deferred — currently documented only inline in the Highlight spec; worth a cross-cutting foundation PR once tables/tags-input/toaster land
ars-derive (proc-macro crate) has no coverage measurement path via either llvm-cov or xtask coverage wasm — acknowledged as unmeasurable, not a gap to fix
